content: 2 Overview During the summer vacation of Sophomore Year, I got the opportunity to join the Machine Intelligence Laboratory (MiLAB) in Westlake University as a visiting student for 8 month (from July 2022 to March 2023). MiLAB is founded Dr. Donglin Wang. They conduct the pioneering research of Robot Learning, with the mission to endow real robot with high flexibility, fast adaptation and autonomous learning by making use of machine learning algorithms. The specific topics they focus on are: 1) Deep Reinforcement Learning Theory; 2) Deep Meta-Learning Theory; 3) Robot Behavior Intelligence. Upon being assigned to the Quadruped Blind Guiding Robot project by Dr. Wang, I conducted the systematic review on the related works. After I restored the algorithm codes from previous works, I found the issues of the existed blind guiding systems. To solve the problem, we proposed a data-driven guiding system to cope with the effect brighten by individual differences among users. We compared the performance of our models with previous works and achieve significant performance improvements. Our guiding system was deployed in both simulation and real quadruped robots. Finally, I composed our works to a research paper, and submitted it to IEEE Conference.
title: [Academics] Quadruped Blind Guiding Robot

content: 2 Overview The Underwater Grabbing Robot is designed for 2021 National Innovation Training Project for University. I was responsible for the circuit system design, control algorithm design, and visual servoing algorithm design of the robot. Unlike traditional frame-based underwater grabbing robots, this robot features a four-axis olive-shaped structure that effectively reduces fluid resistance, giving it high maneuverability. The bottom gripper is capable of visual servoing to grasp target objects. The robot’s motion control algorithm utilizes a cascaded dual-loop PID controller. The object detection algorithm employs YOLOv5, with the dataset sourced from the OpenImages open-source dataset.
title: [Academics] Vision-Servo Underwater Gripping Robot

content: 3 Required Knowledge Robotics Computer Vision Robotic Manipulation Electronic Devices and Circuit Theory Classic Control Theory.
title: [Academics] Vision-Servo Underwater Gripping Robot

content: 2 Overview The Vision Servo Agricultural Quadcopter is the project I designed for National Undergraduate Electronics Design Contest. It ultilizes TM4C123G as the main controller. By using inertial sensors, laser ranging sensors, and optical flow sensors for perception, the UAV enables closed-loop control. Additionally, I employed a camera for visual servo. The control algorithm is based on a dual-loop PID controller, allowing for stable flight and good maneuverability. It is capable of functions such as color block tracking, line following, precision landing, and laser shooting.
title: [Academics] Vision-Servo Agricultural Quadcopter

content: 3 Required Knowledge Classic Control Theory Computer Vision
title: [Academics] Vision-Servo Agricultural Quadcopter
